concept: Localize a cytotoxic payload to target cells by covalently attaching a targeting ligand (a small molecule such as folate, or an antibody) through a cleavable linker; the payload is released after receptor-mediated uptake.
mechanism: Folate-receptor-alpha (over-expressed on many tumors) binds the folate-conjugated payload and mediates internalization; the cleavable spacer then releases the cytotoxin inside the target cell. Antibody variant: an anti-antigen antibody delivers the payload via a protease-cleavable peptide linker.
precedent_drug: Vintafolide (EC145; folate-vinca conjugate). Antibody-conjugate variant: brentuximab vedotin.

citation.label: Ambrosio AJ, Suzin D, Palmer JL, et al. Vintafolide (EC145) for the treatment of folate-receptor-alpha positive platinum-resistant ovarian cancer. Expert Rev Clin Pharmacol. 2014;7(4):443-50.
citation.note: Antibody-conjugate variant: Francisco JA et al., Blood. 2003;102(4):1458-65 (brentuximab vedotin / cAC10-vcMMAE), PMID 12714494 | DOI 10.1182/blood-2003-01-0039.
